title: HeadPlay: An Auto-Regressive World Model for Playable Human Head Avatars
teaser: Build a small-scale Genie/WHAM-style world model for a human head: a transformer that takes camera motion and facial expression as actions and hallucinates the next frame, token by token, on a single 10 GB GPU.
abstract: Recent foundation world models such as Genie 3 [Genie 3] and Microsoft's WHAM/Muse [WHAM, WHAMM] turn a video game into a playable neural simulation by training a transformer on interleaved sequences of image and action tokens. This project transplants the same recipe into the most expressive constrained domain we have access to in the lab: a single (or few) human head(s). The student first builds a synthetic dataset of paired (camera trajectory, expression sequence, frame) tuples by driving a pre-trained Gaussian Head Avatar [GaussianHeadAvatar, GaussianAvatars] on top of the NeRSemble multi-view capture dataset [NeRSemble]. A discrete frame tokenizer (FSQ [FSQ]) is trained to encode head frames into a compact codebook that is by construction immune to collapse. A small autoregressive transformer is then trained to predict the next image/action token, with MaskGIT-style [MaskGIT] parallel decoding for interactive frame rates at inference. The headline research question is identity drift over long rollouts: the student investigates a spatial latent memory [SpatialMemory] that conditions on identity-bearing FLAME shape parameters and/or on a small bank of episodic frames, so that the head stays the same head a hundred frames in. Resolution is intentionally kept low (64²–128²); the goal is not photorealism but a clean, end-to-end study of the world-model recipe inside a tightly constrained data distribution, on consumer hardware.
milestones: Build the synthetic data pipeline: render camera trajectories paired with FLAME expression sequences through a pre-trained Gaussian Head Avatar; produce labelled (frame, camera, expression) tuples.

Prototype end-to-end: train an FSQ tokenizer on the rendered frames, then a small autoregressive transformer on interleaved image/action tokens for a single identity. Iterate visually until short rollouts are convincing.

Set up the evaluation harness before scaling: FVD and PSNR/LPIPS for image fidelity, ArcFace cosine similarity vs. time for identity drift, and an inverse-dynamics model to measure action-following accuracy (à la MineWorld [MineWorld]).
Ablate the spatial latent memory: with vs. without FLAME-shape conditioning, with vs. without an episodic frame bank; quantify long-rollout consistency.
Scale: add MaskGIT decoding for real-time inference, then push along whichever axis the budget allows — higher resolution, more identities, or an out-of-distribution test on Unreal Engine MetaHumans.
